# Ranielli Montagna

> Full Stack Software Engineer at Lemon Energia, from Brazil, specializing in React, React Native, Next.js, Node.js and TypeScript. Portfolio, blog and open source projects.

Ranielli Montagna (also known as Rani Montagna or Ranni Montagna) is a Brazilian Full Stack Software Engineer currently working at Lemon Energia. He has 5+ years of experience building scalable web and mobile applications and previously worked at Luizalabs, the technology division of Magazine Luiza, developing products for physical store operations that impacted inventory and logistics workflows across 1,000+ stores and 1,000+ stock clerks.

## Identity

- Full name: Ranielli Montagna
- Also known as: Rani Montagna, Ranni Montagna, Ranielli
- Nationality: Brazilian
- Location: Brazil
- Role: Full Stack Software Engineer
- Current company: Lemon Energia
- Current experience: Lemon Energia — engineering at the bridge between the Center of Excellence and business areas, with a focus on digital solutions, automation and pragmatic AI
- GitHub: https://github.com/RanielliMontagna
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/rannimontagna
- Twitter/X: https://twitter.com/rannimontagna

## Skills

- Frontend: React, Next.js, TypeScript, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS, React Native, Micro Frontends
- Backend: Node.js, REST APIs, GraphQL, PostgreSQL
- Tools & practices: Figma, Git, Docker, CI/CD, Testing, Accessibility, Design Systems, AI Automation, Model Context Protocol
- Languages: Portuguese (native), English (professional)

## Portfolio

- [Portfolio & Home](https://ranimontagna.com): Main portfolio page of Ranielli Montagna with about, experience, skills and contact sections
- [Blog](https://ranimontagna.com/blog): Technical blog with articles on React, Next.js, Node.js, TypeScript and web development best practices
- [Projects](https://ranimontagna.com/projects): Open source projects and GitHub repositories by Ranielli Montagna

## Blog

The blog at https://ranimontagna.com/blog contains technical articles authored by Ranielli Montagna covering:
- React and Next.js development
- Node.js and backend architecture
- TypeScript patterns and best practices
- UI/UX design for developers
- Web performance and modern tooling

For a full list of blog posts with descriptions, see: https://ranimontagna.com/llms-full.txt
